arts.ttl: England and Spain draw as respect shines through on path to progress
arts.athr: Sid Lowe at Estadio Municipal de La Línea de la Concepción
arts.sum: Stars of tomorrow, including under-21 debutant Jobe Bellingham, swap shirts and stories in goalless friendly Dean Huijsen revived an old tradition here. When was the last time you saw a footballer not just swap shirts with his opponent but immediately put it on too? As the final whistle went, the Bournemouth defender who was born in Amsterdam but represents Spain where he was raised, made straight for Samuel Iling-Junior who, like him, had played at Juventus. He moved like a young man on a mission, which it turned out he was. They embraced and when Huijsen finally walked off he was wearing white, a No 14 on the back. It had been that kind of night, a familiarity and a sort of mutual admiration about the place, something of themselves seen in each other. Something shared, and not just, as it turned out, the scoreline. At the end, ball boys holding camera phones had run on seeking out Jobe Bellingham, who had played over an hour on his U21 debut, while players from each side made their way towards familiar faces, of which there were many. This was a friendly that was, well, friendly. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Noni Madueke’s Greece audition is latest act in England coming-of-age story
arts.sum: Winger’s confident display on first England start shows he is now ready to provide competition for Bukayo Saka Noni Madueke has a particular way of explaining things away. Direct, confident, a little dismissive – rather like how he plays. The Chelsea winger’s apology after the Wolverhampton affair at the start of the season featured a number of choice cuts. “I just want to apologise to everyone that I might have offended,” Madueke said, after accidentally sharing a private message about Wolverhampton being a “shit place” with his followers on social media. “It wasn’t meant to be out on my socials like that. I’m sure Wolverhampton is a nice town and I’m sorry.” Continue reading...

arts.ttl: 'A night of redemption for Carsley offers real hope for Tuchel'
arts.sum: The finest display of England interim manager Lee Carsley's time in charge sends a positive message to incoming coach Thomas Tuchel.

arts.ttl: UK police to investigate treatment of England fans
arts.sum: Local police in Athens are said to have used riot shields and tear gas to control England fans before the Nations League fixture with Greece.

arts.ttl: 'Love it': Dominic Solanke and Jack Grealish wowed by Aston Villa player's international display
arts.sum: Aston Villa forward Morgan Rogers made his England debut on Thursday night and helped his nation run out 3-0 winners over Greece in the Nations...

arts.ttl: Carsley: Kane will start vs Rep. Ireland - 'I didn't drop him'
arts.sum: But Carsley says Kane will start in the final group game - and insisted Thursday's selection was simply an opportunity to give goal scorer Ollie Watkins more big-match experience for England. England interim boss Lee Carsley has confirmed Harry Kane will start against Republic of Ireland in the Nations League at Wembley on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Lee Carsley urges England to follow Germany and Spain’s trust in youth
arts.sum: Interim manager backs promotion of under-21 players Carsley: youngsters who faced Greece ‘in good position’ Lee Carsley believes England will have a better chance of glory if they follow Germany and Spain in promoting players who have won trophies at youth level. Carsley, who steps aside for Thomas Tuchel after Sunday’s home game against the Republic of Ireland, has not been afraid to bring in inexperienced players during his spell as interim head coach. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Lee Carsley clarifies Harry Kane situation after England omission - "Far from it"
arts.sum: England beat Greece convincingly in Athens to wrestle back top spot in their Nations League group - and did so with captain and record scorer Harry Kane on the bench

arts.ttl: Jude Bellingham sparks fresh England captaincy debate as Harry Kane dealt reality check
arts.sum: England cruised to a 3-0 Nations League victory over Greece in Athens on Thursday night with captain Harry Kane on the bench and Jude Bellingham running the show in midfield

arts.ttl: Lee Carsley believes exciting times lie ahead for England under Thomas Tuchel
arts.sum: The new boss cannot fail to have been impressed as he watched Thursday’s 3-0 win in Greece from afar.
